calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eliza X. Wang for petitioner Na Li (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Waiver of Account and Report of Administrator and Petition for its Settlement, and for Final Distribution.

The Court inquired whether Na Li had consulted with an attorney before signing the assignment. Ms. Wang confirmed that Na Li's brother, a Chinese national residing in China, had consulted both an attorney and a CPA in the United States concerning tax implications, as the decedent's intent had been to retain the property rather than liquidate it. The brother ultimately decided to assign his interest to his sister after considering various legal and financial factors, including declining property values and the risks associated with foreign ownership.

The Court noted that the petition was silent regarding statutory fees. Ms. Wang stated that she had waived attorney's fees on a pro bono basis, and that costs had been paid directly by her client. She explained that this was a simple petition with minimal work and that she had taken the matter as a favor for a friend.

Following discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Final Distribution

Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Continued To Jun-09-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Lawrence Adamsky, specially appearing for attorney Eliza Wang, for Na Li, Administrator (via Zoom).

Mr. Adamsky requested a 60-day continuance to allow time to file a petition for final distribution. The court continued the matter to June 9, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
